text.title: Diary Studies Core Rules
text.summary: 5 testable rules anchoring the diary-studies methodology, each grounded in a source.

rule.statement: Reminder cadence MUST be scheduled (push notification + SMS fallback); studies without reminders MUST be rejected.
rule.rationale: Participation drops 40-60% by day 3 without reminders; the cadence is the methodology's reliability lever.

rule.statement: Study MUST offer both daily-reflection (fixed time) and event-triggered entries; single-mode studies miss complementary signal.
rule.rationale: Daily entries capture habits; event-triggered capture incidents; both are required for full coverage.

rule.statement: Each user segment MUST have >=8 active participants completing >=70% of expected entries; segments below the threshold MUST be excluded from analysis.
rule.rationale: <8 produces noisy themes; <70% completion produces sparse longitudinal data.

rule.statement: Every entry MUST carry timestamp + context tag (location, app state); untimestamped entries MUST be discarded.
rule.rationale: Time + context are the longitudinal dimension; entries without them collapse to interview data.

rule.statement: Retention period, anonymisation, and deletion rights MUST be explicit in consent; indefinite retention is forbidden.
rule.rationale: Diary data is intimate; GDPR + IRB require explicit terms.

rule.statement: If the Skip-If preconditions hold, the methodology MUST be skipped and the caller routed to the linked alternative.
rule.rationale: Applying this machinery to the wrong workflow burns tokens without producing a usable artefact.
